title: Bad Bunny Makes History at 2026 Grammys
text: Bad Bunny created a landmark moment at the 68th Grammy Awards on February 1, 2026, when his album DeBí TiRAR MáS FOToS won Album of the Year—the first Spanish-language album ever to receive this top honor. The Puerto Rican artist became visibly emotional upon hearing his name announced by presenter Harry Styles. While the audience stood applauding, Bad Bunny remained seated, covering his eyes as tears streamed down his face. He continued wiping away tears as he walked to the podium. During his acceptance speech, he dedicated the award to those who had to leave their homelands to pursue their dreams. He expressed gratitude toward his native Puerto Rico and his family. Bad Bunny secured two additional Grammy awards that evening: Best Global Music Performance and Best Música Urbana Album. The 31-year-old declined to perform at the ceremony due to contractual obligations for the 2026 Super Bowl Halftime Show. When host Trevor Noah asked about this requirement, Bad Bunny acknowledged the constraint, admitting, "I came here and I thought I was performing...but now I can't."
